evidence.scientific_clarity: Diagram faithfully implements the brief: solid arrows for data flow, dashed for KB query/feedback, Retrieval and Generation groups with rounded-rect backgrounds, clear top-to-bottom pipeline.
evidence.typography: Script never sets pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=0; PDF embeds Type3 (DejaVuSans, DejaVuSans-Bold) — the matplotlib default — so text is not selectable/editable.
evidence.palette: Pastel blue/green/yellow/purple are reasonably colorblind-safe; blue reserved for input and green for output (directional discipline), no red/green conflict; not explicitly CB-validated.
evidence.layout_density: Six nodes spread across a 7×6.5 in canvas with clear vertical hierarchy; spacing is adequate but the canvas has noticeable vertical padding above/below the diagram.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption accurately describes all components and arrow semantics; legend is present (lower-right); legend merges Input and Output under one 'Input/Output' entry despite them having distinct colors.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF is vector for shapes/arrows but fonts are Type3 (subtype Type3 in PDF strings); fonttype=42 not set, so text is not selectable or editable in Illustrator/Inkscape.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (20 KB), figure.png (60 KB), figure.svg (37 KB), caption.tex (575 B), and gen_figure.py (5.7 KB) all exist and are non-trivial; SVG is a bonus.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names every component, explains color coding (blue Query, green Generated Sample), and distinguishes solid vs dashed arrows; lacks a quantitative claim, but none is applicable for a pure architecture diagram.

overall_strengths: Architecture diagram fully satisfies the brief: correct grouping, directional solid/dashed arrow semantics, and a clean top-to-bottom pipeline layout.
All required output files (PDF, PNG, caption.tex, gen_figure.py) are present and non-trivial; SVG is also produced as a bonus.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 is never set, so the PDF embeds Type3 (bitmapped-outline) DejaVu Sans fonts — text is not selectable or editable, a standard journal requirement.
DejaVu Sans is the matplotlib default and is not preferred for publication; the legend conflates Input and Output nodes under a single entry despite their distinct colors.
